design_contract.description: Two-stage architecture workflow: design first, then translate the approved design into Minecraft BuildPlan parts.

coordinate_system.origin: Use [0, 64, 0] in BuildPlan. Local x=width, y=height, z=depth.
coordinate_system.bounds: Every module owns an inclusive local bounding box [from] -> [to].
coordinate_system.grid: Choose a structural grid before parts: bay width, floor height, roof/eave offsets, and symmetry axis.
coordinate_system.units: Minecraft blocks are the final construction units. Use block counts as the dimension source of truth.

design_spec_fields.building_type: pagoda, temple_hall, water_town, office_tower, landmark_gate, stone_bridge, suspension_bridge, custom
design_spec_fields.selected_template: template key from templates.json or custom
design_spec_fields.grid: bay widths, floor heights, tier heights, bridge spans, or other repeat units
design_spec_fields.modules: ordered modules with name, bounding_box, role, materials, and interface faces
design_spec_fields.interfaces: connection faces between modules, e.g. tower_left.top == skybridge.left_support
design_spec_fields.minecraft_translation: how real design elements become box/window/stairs/slab/component parts
design_spec_fields.quality_checks: checks to run after generation, such as octagonal footprint, clear void, walkable bridge deck, repeated lighting
design_spec_fields.performance_budget: max block count, preview block count, tick-command budget, animation flag, suggested view distance, and minimum server memory

module_schema.name: short stable module id
materials: palette keys or minecraft block ids

interfaces.north: optional expected neighbor or open facade
interfaces.south: optional expected neighbor or open facade
interfaces.east: optional expected neighbor or open facade
interfaces.west: optional expected neighbor or open facade

interface_schema.note: short explanation of the required cut-face alignment

notes: prefer static lights unless animation is explicitly requested

split_generation_rules: Split only by modules with explicit bboxes and shared interface faces.
All module bboxes must be in the same local coordinate system.
Adjacent modules should either overlap by one structural block or meet exactly on a named interface face.
Void/air clearing modules must run before facade/interior/detail modules that occupy the same area.
Keep floors, facade grids, and bridges aligned to the same floor_height or bay_width.
For repeated modules, define one component or pattern and place it with parameter changes instead of writing unrelated copies.
when_to_split_llm_calls: Large landmarks over roughly 120k visible blocks.
Buildings with distinct structural systems, such as twin towers plus skybridge plus podium.
Scenes with independent ancient buildings, bridges, streets, and landscape pieces.
Any build where one prompt cannot reliably preserve dimensions, interfaces, and material schedule.
